name: June Christy
abstract: June Christy (born Shirley Luster; November 20, 1925 – June 21, 1990) was an American singer, known for her work in the cool jazz genre and for her silky smooth vocals. Her success as a singer began with The Stan Kenton Orchestra.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music

works.t: Babes in Arms: My Funny Valentine

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: I was with that band for about a year and a half including traveling to Europe where we’d do two concerts a day frequently where they would present him with a huge bottle of brandy and they would sit in the back of the bus: Zoot, June Christy, and whoever else and get rid of that usually.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: So what happened with Peter was he had been hired to play with June Christy... Peter shows up... sight reads all of June’s stuff perfectly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe LaBarbera interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 15, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Igoe
about_quotes.evidence: It was Stan Kenton’s band and Shorty Rogers and Shelly Manne, Art Tatum Trio, June Christy, Lennie — oh I can’t think of his name
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Parker and June Christy on the front line... He would play, and then he would back her when she sang.
